name: Decadent Chocolate Cherry Fruitcake
recipeIngredient: Nut or date quick-bread mix, 1 package
Maraschino cherries, drained and cut in half, 10 oz
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch 12/18/95
To prepare fruitcake:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Grease and flour the bottom of one 8-by-4-inch loaf pan, one 9-by-5-inch loaf pan, or three 3-by-5-inch loaf pans.
3. In a large bowl, combine quick-bread mix, pecans, cherries, chocolate chips, water (3/4 cup), kirsch, oil, and egg. Stir until the mixture is moistened.
4. Pour into prepared pan(s).
5. Bake in 350-degree oven for 65 to 80 minutes in an 8-by-4-inch pan, 60 to 70 minutes in a 9-by-5-inch pan, or 40 to 50 minutes in 3-by-5-inch pans until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
6. Let cool 15 minutes, then remove from the pan and let cool completely.
To prepare glaze:
1. In a small saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate chips and oil, stirring until smooth.
2. Drizzle over cake and refrigerate to allow glaze to set. For best flavor, refrigerate glazed cake at least 24 hours before serving.
This cake may be wrapped well in plastic wrap or foil and stored in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or frozen for up to 3 months. Yield: 16 servings.
Note: 2 teaspoons almond extract plus 1/4 cup water can be substituted for the kirsch.
